title: Patchouli and sweet orange soy candle
subtitle: A container soy candle with patchouli and sweet orange essential oils
excerpt: Patchouli and sweet orange is a classic pairing: the warm, earthy depth of patchouli against the bright citrus note of sweet orange. Both are available as essential oils that perform reliably in soy candles. Fragrance load at 6%. A single cotton wick in a ceramic or glass container.
sourceNotes: Essential oil performance in soy candles: Candlescience technical reference (2022). Patchouli at 4% and sweet orange at 2% in container candle (total 6% of wax weight): within IFRA category 12 candle guidelines. Sweet orange EO flash point 45 to 50 degrees C; add at or below 60 degrees C to avoid significant loss.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Pour multiple jars from the same batch for a consistent result.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 48 hours after pouring before burning for the fragrance to set fully in the soy wax.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: The candle keeps several years. Citrus essential oils fade faster than woody base notes; the patchouli will outlast the orange in the fragrance profile over time. Keep the lid on when not in use.

glossaryTerms.definition: The temperature at which wax is poured. Sweet orange essential oil has a low flash point and should be added to wax that has cooled below 60 degrees C to minimise evaporation.
The first burn sets the pool pattern for all subsequent burns. Burn until the wax pool reaches the full diameter of the container.

content.text: Patchouli provides a warm, mossy base note that carries well in a room; sweet orange adds a clean brightness that lifts the blend without dominating. In soy wax, both perform well if poured at the right temperature. Sweet orange essential oil is volatile and should be added at 55 to 60 degrees C rather than at the higher temperature used for some fragrance oils. The patchouli base note lingers; the orange is more forward when the candle is freshly made.

items.prepNote: 4% of wax weight; add at 55 to 60 degrees C
2% of wax weight; add at 55 to 60 degrees C
ECO-4 or CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm jar

content.text: Melt wax over a water bath only. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach.

Melt the soy wax to 75 degrees C. Remove from heat and allow to cool to 55 to 60 degrees C before adding the essential oils. Add the patchouli first, then the sweet orange, and stir for 2 minutes. Adding the citrus EO below 60 degrees C reduces the amount that evaporates before the wax sets.
Centre the wick with a wick bar. Pour at the
of 55 to 60 degrees C for this recipe, slightly lower than a fragrance-oil soy candle. Leave undisturbed for 8 hours. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
rule: burn until the wax pool reaches the full jar diameter, around 2 to 3 hours for a 6 cm jar.
Keep the lid on when not in use to slow fragrance loss. Store away from heat and light. Keeps several years; the citrus note fades faster than the patchouli base over time.
